title: Fix HTTP 413 payload too large error on invite publish

requirements.text: Implement a chunked image storage strategy in the Motoko backend to work around the 2MB IC ingress message limit. Instead of accepting full Base64 image data inline in createInvite/updateInvite calls, add a dedicated uploadImageChunk(inviteId, fieldKey, chunkIndex, totalChunks, chunkData) endpoint that accepts image data in chunks (each chunk ≤ 1MB). Add a finalizeImage(inviteId, fieldKey) endpoint that assembles the chunks and stores the complete image against the invite record. The invite record's image fields (bridePhoto, groomPhoto, coverPhoto, galleryImages array entries) should store the assembled data internally after finalization. createInvite and updateInvite payloads must no longer carry raw image data — image fields in those payloads should accept only an empty/placeholder value or be omitted entirely.

quotes: Request 0x169df3a2e892d669293bfc5e2c99950f322cca801fbf7d400b84e87ec5f350b2 is too large. Message byte size 2794141 is larger than the max allowed 2097152.

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes an uploadImageChunk endpoint that accepts inviteId, fieldKey (bridePhoto | groomPhoto | coverPhoto | galleryImage:{index}), chunkIndex, totalChunks, and a Blob/Text chunk payload.
Backend exposes a finalizeImage endpoint that reassembles chunks for a given inviteId + fieldKey and stores the result in the invite record.
createInvite and updateInvite no longer accept raw Base64 image payloads; calling them with large image data no longer triggers a 413.
After finalization, getInvite returns the assembled image data correctly for each field.
Chunk reassembly handles out-of-order delivery by using chunkIndex ordering.

requirements.text: Update the frontend photo upload flow to use the new chunked upload endpoints instead of embedding Base64 data inline in the publish payload. In StepPhotoUpload.tsx and StepGalleryMusic.tsx, after a user selects an image file, split the Base64 data URL into chunks of at most 1MB each and call uploadImageChunk sequentially for each chunk, then call finalizeImage to commit the image. Store only a reference token or the fieldKey (not the full Base64 string) in the creator form state. When the CreateInvitePage assembles the final InvitePayload for createInvite/updateInvite, omit raw image data — images are already stored via the chunked upload flow. Show per-image upload progress during chunked upload.

quotes: Message byte size 2794141 is larger than the max allowed 2097152.

acceptanceCriteria: Selecting and uploading a bride, groom, or cover photo triggers chunked upload calls to the backend and finalizes the image before the user proceeds.
Gallery image uploads also use chunked upload, with per-image progress indication.
The publish payload sent via createInvite/updateInvite no longer contains raw Base64 image strings.
Publishing an invite with multiple photos no longer returns an HTTP 413 error.
Published /invite/:id pages still display all uploaded photos correctly after the chunked upload approach.
Upload progress UI is shown per image during chunk transmission.

constraints: Each individual message to the IC canister must remain strictly under 2MB (2,097,152 bytes).
Do not change immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor (backend/main.mo).
nonGoals: Compressing or resizing uploaded images on the client side.
Storing images in any external storage service.
Changing the visual design or template styling.
Modifying the RSVP form or events timeline.
